name: shiv (Build Self-Contained Python Zipapps)

performance.notes: First run extracts to ~/.shiv cache; subsequent runs fast (~100ms startup)

agent_readiness.notes: LinkedIn's tool for building self-contained Python zipapps. shiv -o app.pyz -e mypackage:main -r requirements.txt. shiv -c 'import mypackage; mypackage.main()' -o app.pyz. Output: single .pyz file containing all dependencies. First run: extracts to ~/.shiv/cache/hash/. Subsequent runs use cache. Target must have Python installed. Lighter than PyInstaller — Python must exist on target. pip: pip install shiv. Used at LinkedIn for internal tools. Unlike PyInstaller: does not bundle Python interpreter.

gotchas: Target machine must have Python installed (not fully self-contained)
First run is slow — extracts to ~/.shiv cache
Cache may cause issues if multiple versions deployed to same machine
Not suitable for distributing to end users without Python knowledge
best_when: Internal CLI tools deployment to servers with Python installed
DevOps/ops tooling distributed to developers without virtualenv setup
When PyInstaller's bundled Python interpreter is overkill
Deploying Python scripts as single-file executables to Linux servers
avoid_when: End-user distribution without guaranteed Python install
When PyInstaller or Nuitka are needed for fully self-contained binaries
summary: shiv builds self-contained Python zipapps (.pyz files). shiv -o app.pyz -e mypackage:main -r requirements.txt. Single file contains all dependencies. First run extracts to ~/.shiv cache; subsequent runs use cache. Python must be installed on target. BSD-2 by LinkedIn. Simpler than PyInstaller for server/internal tooling distribution where Python is available. Not for end-user distribution without Python.
